title: Why Modern Neural Networks Overconfide
concept: Modern deep networks are prone to overconfidence due to their high capacity, batch normalization, and training on cross-entropy loss.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the difference between model accuracy and model calibration?

options.text: Accuracy checks how often the model is correct; calibration checks if the model knows how likely it is to be correct.
Accuracy only works on training data.
Calibration only works on grayscale images.

exercises.explain: Correct! A model can be highly accurate but poorly calibrated, or moderately accurate but perfectly calibrated.

cards.title: Deeper but more arrogant
cards.body: Older, shallow neural networks from the 1990s (like LeNet) had poor accuracy but were actually well-calibrated. Modern deep networks (like ResNet) are incredibly accurate, but they are also notoriously overconfident. Why did this happen?

cards.title: The source of overconfidence
cards.body: Modern networks overconfide because they are trained to minimize Cross-Entropy Loss, which pushes logits to infinity to reach zero loss. The network's high capacity, combined with regularizers like Batch Normalization and lack of weight decay, allows it to memorize answers and inflate its confidence.

cards.title: Cramming for a test
cards.body: Imagine a student who crams for a test by memorizing the exact phrasing of practice questions. On the test, they confidently bubble in answers instantly without truly understanding. They feel 100% confident, but their actual knowledge is full of gaps.

cards.title: ResNet-110 vs. LeNet-5
cards.body: In a famous 2017 study, LeNet-5 had an average error of 27% and average confidence of 27% (perfect calibration). ResNet-110 had an error of only 6%, but its average confidence was 95% (severe overconfidence!).

cards.title: Regularization doesn't solve calibration
cards.body: You might think modern regularizations like Batch Normalization and Weight Decay, which prevent overfitting on accuracy, must also help calibration. In reality, Batch Normalization is a major cause of miscalibration because it distributes features in a way that inflates final sigmoid/softmax scores.

exercises.prompt: Why does training on Cross-Entropy loss lead to overconfident models?
exercises.explain: Cross-Entropy loss is never truly satisfied; it continually pushes the target logit higher and higher, inflating confidence.

options.text: Because it only reaches zero loss when the correct class probability is exactly 1.0, pushing logits to infinity.
Because it deletes the gradients of wrong classes.
Because it forces the model to use Sigmoid instead of Softmax.
Because it decreases the network capacity.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Batch Normalization helps prevent overfitting and improves confidence calibration in deep networks.
exercises.explain: False. While Batch Normalization improves accuracy, research shows it actually worsens confidence calibration.
Modern models suffer from overconfidence, predicting near 100% confidence even on incorrect guesses.
exercises.sentence: Modern deep neural networks are highly accurate, but tend to be severely ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these factors contributes most to modern model overconfidence?
exercises.explain: High capacity and Batch Normalization allow deep networks to fit data closely, inflating logits and confidence.

options.text: High model capacity combined with Batch Normalization
Training on small datasets without any normalizations

exercises.prompt: You are explaining to a product manager why a newly trained ResNet is stating 99.9% confidence when misclassifying an orange as a tennis ball. Which explanation is correct?

options.text: The model has high capacity and was trained to minimize Cross-Entropy, which continually pushes target probabilities toward 1.0, making it overconfident on visually similar errors.
The model has run out of memory, causing it to default to the tennis ball class.
The learning rate was set to zero during training.
The orange was painted yellow, changing its convolutional filters.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Deep networks optimize for absolute certainty to satisfy Cross-Entropy loss. This capacity to fit data results in high logits and exaggerated confidence, even on incorrect predictions. Next, let's look at how we fix this using temperature scaling!
